name: claude-plugin
description: **Use MCP repo-map tools when:** - Searching for symbols by name pattern (faster than Grep) - Getting all symbols in a file (faster than Read + parsing)
capabilities: Fast symbol search for functions, classes, and methods using pre-built SQLite index
Get all symbols defined in a specific file with one query
Retrieve exact source code for any symbol by name
Automatic background indexing with status monitoring tools
Project manifest generation showing structure and build commands
Duplicate code detection in repo map documentation
author: ChipFlow
securitySummary: This is a documentation/help skill for the Context Tools MCP plugin. All 89 static findings are FALSE_POSITIVE. The skill contains only markdown documentation describing CLI tools and file paths. No actual code execution, network calls, or data exfiltration. File operations are scoped to the .claude/ directory within user projects.
valueStatement: This skill adds MCP tools for fast symbol search across Python, C++, and Rust codebases. It automatically indexes functions, classes, and methods into a local SQLite database, enabling quick lookups without full codebase scans.
userTitle: Index code symbols for faster navigation
limitations: Only supports Python, C++, and Rust file symbol indexing
Symbol search requires explicit pattern matching, not natural language
Learnings file must be created manually if needed
Session restart required after plugin installation for MCP tools

useCases.title: Navigate complex codebases
useCases.description: Quickly find and jump to specific functions, classes, or methods without scanning entire project directories.

useCases.title: Identify duplicate code
useCases.description: Detect duplicated functions and classes across the codebase to reduce maintenance burden and improve code quality.

useCases.title: Understand project structure
useCases.description: Generate project manifest and repo map to quickly grasp architecture, build commands, and code organization.

promptTemplates.title: Find function
promptTemplates.scenario: Search for a function by name pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for all functions matching the pattern 'handle_*' in the codebase using the repo-map MCP tool.

promptTemplates.title: Get class methods
promptTemplates.scenario: List all methods in a class
promptTemplates.prompt: Get all symbols defined in the file 'src/api/handlers/user_handler.py' using the repo-map MCP tool.

promptTemplates.title: View function source
promptTemplates.scenario: View exact source code of a symbol
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the source code for the function 'parse_user_config' so I can see its implementation details.

promptTemplates.title: Check indexing status
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the repo map status and show me how many symbols have been indexed in this project.

bestPractices: Use symbol search patterns like '*Handler' or 'get_*' to find related functions quickly
Run reindex after adding new files to keep the symbol database current
Check repo_map_status when tools return errors to diagnose indexing issues
antiPatterns: Using symbol search for content-based searches instead of Grep tool
Expecting natural language queries like 'find the login function' instead of using patterns
Forgetting to restart the session after plugin installation, then wondering why tools are unavailable

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported for symbol indexing?
faq.answer: Python, C++, and Rust files are supported. The indexer extracts functions, classes, and methods.

faq.question: How does the dynamic directory support work?
faq.answer: MCP tools automatically adapt to the current working directory. Each project gets its own .claude/repo-map.db file.

faq.question: Is my code data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. All indexing and search happens locally on your machine. No data is transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why do I need to restart after installing?
faq.answer: MCP servers start when Claude Code launches. Plugin changes only take effect on the next session startup.

faq.question: What happens if indexing gets stuck?
faq.answer: The watchdog detects hung processes after 10 minutes and automatically resets status. Use reindex_repo_map to recover.

faq.question: How is this different from using Grep or Search tools?
faq.answer: Symbol search uses a pre-built SQLite index for faster lookups. Use symbol search for names, Grep for file content.
